entries.summary: The regime is Late-Cycle/Stagflationary Transition: the economy is expanding at +2% real GDP with ISM Manufacturing at 52.7, but inflation is re-accelerating on Iran energy shock and tariff pass-through, the equity risk premium has flipped negative (-58bps), and markets have fully priced out 2026 Fed cuts. This portfolio expresses that regime through five complementary exposures: GOOGL and AVGO anchor the AI infrastructure secular thesis across different value-chain points (application/cloud vs. custom silicon), GLD provides the mandatory stagflation and geopolitical tail-risk hedge (the only asset that benefits from both a Treasury market stress event AND an Iran escalation simultaneously), GS capitalizes on the curve re-steepening and elevated FICC volatility that define the late-cycle financial environment, and LLY diversifies the book into a recession-immune secular demand theme completely orthogonal to AI capex and energy dynamics. The dominant risk being underwritten is an AI CapEx Cycle Bust — if Microsoft, Google, or Meta signals material capex deceleration, both GOOGL and AVGO face headwinds simultaneously; GLD, GS, and LLY are specifically sized to offset that correlated drawdown.
entries.diversificationNote: The five picks span four distinct GICS sectors: Communication Services (GOOGL), Information Technology (AVGO), Financials (GS), Health Care (LLY), and a Commodities/Real Assets sleeve (GLD). The accepted factor concentration is two AI-infrastructure names — GOOGL and AVGO — which share secular AI capex exposure but occupy categorically different positions in the value chain (hyperscaler cloud platform vs. custom silicon/XPU designer); GS, LLY, and GLD are explicitly non-correlated to this factor, providing genuine diversification against the dominant portfolio risk.

picks.name: Alphabet Inc. Class A
picks.catalyst: April CPI print (due May 12) plus GOOGL's Google Cloud Next conference pipeline announcements and any Azure/AWS share-shift data in Q2 cloud earnings

picks.name: Broadcom Inc.
picks.catalyst: Broadcom Q2 FY2026 earnings report (expected early June 2026) with XPU revenue run-rate update and FY2027 AI chip revenue guidance

picks.name: SPDR Gold Shares ETF
picks.catalyst: Iran/Strait of Hormuz escalation (military briefing to Trump confirmed); Kevin Warsh Fed Chair confirmation (May 15) signaling hawkish pivot that pressures DXY; April CPI upside print (May 12)

picks.name: Goldman Sachs Group Inc.
picks.catalyst: Q2 2026 earnings (mid-July) with investment banking and FICC trading revenue update; Kevin Warsh confirmation (May 15) signaling hawkish institutional tone that directly benefits fixed-income trading volumes

picks.name: Eli Lilly and Company
picks.catalyst: Phase 3 readout on orforglipron (oral GLP-1 pill) expected H2 2026 — a positive result opens a $50B+ TAM expansion beyond injectable formats and would be the single largest re-rating event for the stock
